# Implementation Tasks: Marketplace Scraper Scripts

**Branch**: `001-marketplace-scraper` | **Date**: 2025-12-23 | **Plan**: specs/001-marketplace-scraper/plan.md
**Generated by**: `/speckit.tasks` command

## Overview

This task breakdown implements a Python CLI tool that fetches Claude marketplace and plugin data from configured sources and generates a curated README.md with organized tables of marketplaces and categorized plugins.

**Total Tasks**: 23 | **Parallel Opportunities**: 8 | **User Stories**: 3

## Implementation Strategy

**MVP Scope**: User Story 1 (README generation) - Core functionality for fetching marketplaces and generating basic README structure.

**Incremental Delivery**: Each user story builds independently on the foundational setup, enabling parallel development and testing.

**Testing Approach**: Unit tests for individual components, integration tests for end-to-end workflows, contract tests for CLI commands.

## Dependencies & Execution Order

```
Setup → Foundational → User Story 1 → User Story 2 → User Story 3 → Polish
   ↑           ↑              ↑            ↑            ↑         ↑
   └─Blocking──┴─Prerequisites┴─Independent─┴─Enhancement─┴─Enhancement─┴─Quality
```

**Parallel Execution Opportunities**:
- Within each user story: Model/service/CLI tasks can run in parallel
- Cross-story: Stories 2 and 3 can partially run in parallel with Story 1
- Test tasks: All test implementations can run in parallel

---

## Phase 1: Setup (Project Initialization)

**Goal**: Establish project structure and dependencies for marketplace scraper development.

**Independent Test**: Verify Python environment, dependencies installed, and basic CLI structure functional.

### Setup Tasks

- [x] T001 Create Python virtual environment and install dependencies in requirements.txt
- [x] T002 Create scripts/ directory structure with subdirectories for models/, utils/, generators/
- [x] T003 Initialize pytest test structure in tests/unit/ and tests/integration/
- [x] T004 Create basic CLI entry point in scripts/marketplace_scraper.py with argument parser
- [x] T005 Set up logging configuration and basic error handling in scripts/marketplace_scraper.py

---

## Phase 2: Foundational (Prerequisites)

**Goal**: Implement core infrastructure components required by all user stories.

**Independent Test**: All foundational components can be imported and basic functionality verified without marketplace data.

- [x] T010 [P] Add JSON parsing and error handling in scripts/utils/fetcher.py
- [x] T011 [P] Implement marketplace data fetching from source URLs in scripts/utils/fetcher.py

### Data Models

- [x] T012 [P] Implement Marketplace model in scripts/models.py with validation rules
- [x] T013 [P] Implement Plugin model in scripts/models.py with validation rules
- [x] T014 [P] Add data validation utilities in scripts/utils/validators.py

## Phase 3: User Story 1 - Generate README from Marketplace Data (Priority: P1)

**Story Goal**: As a project maintainer, I want to run a script that fetches current marketplace and plugin data from configured sources and automatically generates an updated README.md with organized tables of marketplaces and categorized plugins.

**Independent Test**: Script runs with config.yaml, fetches marketplace data, generates README.md with correct structure and marketplace table.

### CLI Command Implementation

- [x] T015 [US1] Implement generate-readme subcommand in scripts/marketplace_scraper.py
- [x] T016 [US1] Add marketplace data fetching integration in scripts/marketplace_scraper.py
- [x] T017 [US1] Implement basic README generation in scripts/generators/readme_generator.py

### Marketplace Processing

- [x] T018 [US1] Add marketplace.json parsing from GitHub repositories in scripts/utils/fetcher.py
- [x] T019 [US1] Implement plugin extraction from marketplace.json in scripts/utils/fetcher.py
- [x] T020 [US1] Add marketplace data aggregation and deduplication in scripts/marketplace_scraper.py

### README Structure

- [x] T021 [US1] Implement table of contents generation in scripts/generators/readme_generator.py
- [x] T022 [US1] Create marketplace table generation in scripts/generators/readme_generator.py
- [x] T023 [US1] Add basic plugin listing in scripts/generators/readme_generator.py

---

## Phase 4: User Story 2 - Configure Sources via YAML (Priority: P2)

**Story Goal**: As a project maintainer, I want to specify marketplace sources in a config.yaml file so I can easily add or remove sources without modifying the script code.

**Independent Test**: Script reads config.yaml with multiple sources, validates URLs, and processes sources in priority order.

### Configuration Enhancement

- [x] T024 [US2] Enhance Config class to support multiple sources array in scripts/config.py
- [x] T025 [US2] Add source priority handling and enable/disable logic in scripts/config.py
- [x] T026 [US2] Implement source iteration logic in scripts/marketplace_scraper.py

### Validation & Error Handling

- [x] T027 [US2] Add error handling for invalid source URLs in scripts/utils/fetcher.py
- [x] T028 [US2] Implement config validation command in scripts/marketplace_scraper.py
- [x] T029 [US2] Add list-sources command implementation in scripts/marketplace_scraper.py

---

## Phase 5: User Story 3 - Categorize Plugins in README (Priority: P3)

**Story Goal**: As a project maintainer, I want plugins automatically organized by categories in the generated README for better discoverability.

**Independent Test**: Generated README shows plugins organized by category headings with proper grouping and navigation.

### Category Processing

- [x] T030 [US3] Add category extraction from plugin data in scripts/marketplace_scraper.py
- [x] T031 [US3] Implement category-based organization in scripts/generators/readme_generator.py
- [x] T032 [US3] Add category section headers generation in scripts/generators/readme_generator.py

### Table Formatting

- [x] T033 [US3] Implement plugin table generation with columns in scripts/generators/readme_generator.py
- [x] T034 [US3] Add marketplace subsections within categories in scripts/generators/readme_generator.py
- [x] T035 [US3] Handle uncategorized plugins placement in scripts/generators/readme_generator.py

---

## Final Phase: Polish & Cross-Cutting Concerns

**Goal**: Add quality improvements, performance optimizations, and comprehensive error handling.

### Quality & Reliability

- [x] T036 Add comprehensive error handling and logging throughout codebase
- [x] T037 Implement data merging for duplicate plugins from multiple sources
- [x] T038 Add performance optimizations for large datasets in scripts/utils/fetcher.py

### Testing & Validation

- [x] T039 Create comprehensive unit tests for all components in tests/unit/
- [x] T040 Create integration tests for end-to-end workflows in tests/integration/
- [x] T041 Add markdown validation for generated output in scripts/generators/readme_generator.py

### Documentation & Maintenance

- [x] T042 Update quickstart.md with working examples
- [x] T043 Create comprehensive documentation and docstrings throughout codebase
- [x] T044 Add performance monitoring and caching for GitHub API calls

---

## Task Execution Guidelines

### Parallel Execution Examples

**Within User Story 1**:
- T018, T019, T020 can run in parallel (different aspects of data fetching)
- T021, T022, T023 can run in parallel (different README sections)

**Cross-Story Dependencies**:
- User Stories 2 and 3 can partially overlap with Story 1 after T017 completes
- All CLI enhancements (Stories 2-3) can run parallel to README generation (Story 1)

### Testing Strategy

**Unit Tests**: Individual functions and classes (pytest)
**Integration Tests**: End-to-end command execution (pytest)
**Contract Tests**: CLI command input/output validation
**Manual Tests**: README generation quality and formatting

### Success Criteria

- **MVP (Story 1)**: Generates README with marketplace table and basic plugin listings
- **Complete (All Stories)**: Full categorization, multi-source support, robust error handling
- **Quality**: All tests pass, comprehensive error handling, performance within goals

### File Path Standards

All tasks specify exact file paths for clear implementation targets. Tasks are designed to be independently implementable with minimal cross-dependencies within each phase.</content>
